<!--
audience: Russell contributors proposing a locked decision
last-reviewed: 2026-04-17
-->

# ADR-NNNN: <short imperative title>

- **Status:** Proposed | Accepted | Superseded by ADR-MMMM | Deprecated
- **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
- **Deciders:** <names / roles>
- **Tags:** <scope keywords, e.g. `mcp`, `journal`, `safety`>
- **Supersedes:** (optional) ADR-XXXX
- **Superseded by:** (optional) ADR-YYYY

## Context

What problem forced this decision? What constraints bound the solution
space? Cite the relevant sections of
[`cybernetic-health-harness.md`](../../cybernetic-health-harness.md) and
any prior ADRs. Keep this section factual; save advocacy for the
Decision section.

## Decision

State the decision in the imperative. One or two paragraphs. If the
decision has sub-parts, enumerate them.

## Consequences

### Positive

- ...

### Negative / accepted costs

- ...

### Neutral

- ...

## Alternatives considered

Each alternative gets a subsection with why it was rejected. "Do
nothing" is always a valid alternative and should be listed
explicitly when relevant.

### Alternative A

Why rejected.

### Alternative B

Why rejected.

## Implementation notes

Anything a future implementer needs to know that is not obvious from
the Decision section. Migration plan, feature flags, flag-day
coordination. Leave empty if not applicable.

## References

- [`cybernetic-health-harness.md` §X.Y](../../cybernetic-health-harness.md)
- Upstream docs / RFCs / papers
- Related issues / PRs
